title: Professional README that sells the vision
description: Rewrite README.md as a marketing-grade document that converts visitors in 30 seconds.

scope.in: README.md rewrite, verify demo.gif, comparison table, quickstart, architecture diagram
scope.out: No new asset creation (use existing demo.gif), no landing page (separate project)

dod.criterion: README.md leads with centered demo.gif and value prop above the fold
Comparison table: gruai vs Devin vs CrewAI vs AutoGen across 5+ dimensions
3-command quickstart that produces a working setup
Technical details in collapsible sections below the fold

tasks.title: Audit current README and draft marketing outline
tasks.user_scenario: Builder reads the current README and creates a structured outline that prioritizes the developer's 30-second decision window.
tasks.scope: Read README.md, verify demo.gif shows pixel-art office with agents. Produce outline with: hero section structure, above-the-fold content, comparison table columns, badge inventory, and mapping of current content (keep/move/delete).

dod.criterion: Outline document exists in project directory with section-by-section plan
Comparison table has at least 5 dimensions with data for gruai vs Devin vs CrewAI vs AutoGen
Every section of current README mapped to keep/move/delete decision

tasks.title: Rewrite README.md as marketing-grade document
tasks.user_scenario: A developer lands on the GitHub repo and within 30 seconds understands what gruai does, why it is different, and how to try it.
tasks.scope: Rewrite README.md following the outline. Hero: GIF centered, one-line value prop, badges. Above fold: 3-command quickstart. Feature highlights. Comparison table. Architecture diagram. Technical details in collapsible sections.

dod.criterion: README.md starts with centered demo.gif, value prop, and badges visible without scrolling
Quickstart section contains exactly 3 shell commands for a working setup
Comparison table present: gruai vs Devin vs CrewAI vs AutoGen across 5+ dimensions
Technical details (hooks, terminal support, scripts) in collapsible <details> sections
No broken image links or badge URLs
